item.title: DotZap 1.1.1
item.description:
# DotZap 1.1.1
The first public release of DotZap — a macOS menu-bar utility that silently deletes Apple/Windows metadata junk (`._*`, `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`, `desktop.ini`, …) the moment external drives create them.
## New
### What it does - **Watches every mounted volume** automatically. Plug a drive in and DotZap starts cleaning within a second; eject it and DotZap stops. - **Cleans nine kinds of metadata junk out of the box**: Apple Double `._*`, `.DS_Store`, `.Spotlight-V100`, `.Trashes`, `.fseventsd`, `.TemporaryItems`, `__MACOSX`, `Thumbs.db`, and `desktop.ini`. Each rule is individually toggleable. - **Custom rules**. Add your own patterns — exact name, prefix, or glob — for anything else you'd rather not see (`*.tmp~`, `.obsidian`, you pick). - **Per-volume control**. Disable cleaning on a specific drive without touching the global setting, or use the per-volume **whitelist** to protect files that match a built-in rule but you actually want to keep (e.g. `*.psd` on a Photoshop drive). - **Clean Now**. Tap the button in a volume's expanded row to do a one-shot recursive scan instead of waiting for the next file event. - **Activity log** with lifetime stats. See exactly what was deleted, when, from where, and how much space you've recovered. Capped at 500 recent entries; lifetime totals persist across launches.
### The interface - **Menu-bar only** — no Dock icon, no main window, no floating clutter. The `sparkles.rectangle.stack` icon brightens when active and dims when paused. - **Left-click** the icon to pause or resume globally. - **Right-click** to open a glass-styled settings panel (frosted vibrancy, rounded corners, subtle stroke — built to feel like a first-party utility). Click anywhere outside it to dismiss. - **Three tabs**: Volumes, Rules, Activity. Plus an **About** tab with version info, a manual *Check for Updates…* button, and a Quit shortcut (⌘Q). - **Launch at Login** toggle in the Volumes tab — DotZap starts itself when you sign in, no Login Items dance required.
### Auto-updates - **Built-in Sparkle updates** with EdDSA-signed releases. New versions get a non-blocking prompt within 24 hours; you can disable auto-checks in the About tab and still trigger manual checks. Every update's signature is verified against a public key embedded at build time before installation.
### Smart about what it can't touch - DotZap **silently skips paths inside system-owned directories** like `.TemporaryItems/`, `.Spotlight-V100/`, `.Trashes/`, and `.fseventsd/`. These are root-owned even on external drives, so deletion would always fail. The first attempt on each protected location is remembered for the session — no retry storm, no log spam. - **Full Disk Access banner** in the Volumes tab walks you through granting the required system permission and re-checks after you click Open Settings.
### Polish - **App icon** rendered from the same SF Symbol used in the menu bar — white glyph on a blue gradient squircle, generated at all 10 macOS sizes (16/32/128/256/512 @ 1x and 2x). - **Compact About tab layout** — every detail fits in one view at the panel's default size; no scrolling required.
## License
DotZap is now licensed under the [GNU General Public License v3.0](https://github.com/Foiler25/DotZap/blob/main/LICENSE). You're free to modify and redistribute your own builds, as long as the source is made available under the same license.
## Install & update
- **Already installed?** DotZap auto-updates — pick *Check for Updates…* in the About tab to grab it now, or wait for the next scheduled check. - **First time?** Download `DotZap-1.1.1.dmg`, open it, drag DotZap to Applications. First launch: right-click → **Open**, then System Settings → **Privacy & Security** → **Open Anyway**. Then grant Full Disk Access via the banner in the Volumes tab.
**Requires macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later.**
